steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$f \left(x\right)= \left(x-14\right) \left(x+19\right)$$Graph it and use the visible intercept, root, or vertex the question asks for. The requested value is $-\frac{5}{2}$.

steps.title: Apply the new function definition
steps.content: A product of two factors $(x-14)(x+19)$ is a parabola opening upward with vertex midway between the roots.

steps.title: Compute the answer
steps.content: The minimum occurs at $x = \frac{14 + (-19)}{2} = \frac{-5}{2} = -2.5$. That matches Choice D — $-\frac{5}{2}$.
